Title: Comparative genomics provides insights into the biogeographic and biochemical diversity of meliaceous species

Common_Genes.Source_Species: Azadirachta indica; Melia azedarach; Toona sinensis; Citrus sinensis
Common_Genes.Source_Species_Latin_Name: Azadirachta indica; Melia azedarach; Toona sinensis; Citrus sinensis
Common_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Azadirachta indica; Melia azedarach; Toona sinensis; Citrus sinensis
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Expanded tandemly duplicated GGT copies are associated with higher S-allylcysteine content and likely contribute to sulphur-containing volatile accumulation in Meliaceae.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: comparative genomics; copy-number/synteny analysis; tissue transcriptome expression analysis; metabolite quantification of S-allylcysteine
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GGT genes underwent lineage-specific expansion in Meliaceae, with 16 copies in neem and 14 in chinaberry on orthologous chromosomes. 2. Tandemly duplicated GGTs showed high expression particularly in stems and leaves. 3. Species ranking of S-allylcysteine content (T. sinensis > neem > chinaberry > citrus) was consistent with GGT copy number, supporting a role in sulphur-containing volatile biosynthesis.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: High expression particularly in stems and leaves.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: High expression particularly in stems and leaves.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: genome sequencing, transcriptome sequencing; Genome Sequence Archive CRA019071; Genome Warehouse PRJCA030181
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: These genes were highlighted through comparative genomics and expression-metabolite association, but no direct enzyme assays for the expanded Meliaceae GGT copies were reported in this study.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Melia azedarach; Nicotiana benthamiana; Escherichia coli
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes acetylated limonoid formation by catalysing mainly C12-OH acetylation, and also C3-OH acetylation, thereby finalizing toosendanin/isotoosendanin biosynthesis in chinaberry.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: WGCNA-guided candidate selection; heterologous expression in E. coli; in vitro enzyme assays; LC-MS/LC-MS-MS product identification; transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; tissue metabolite profiling; kinetic assays; molecular docking/MD simulation; site-directed mutagenesis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MaAT8824 acetylates the C12-OH of compounds 1 and 3 to produce toosendanin (2) and isotoosendanin (4). 2. It also catalyses acetylation of compound 5 to form compound 6 and can further acetylate compound 6 at C3-OH to produce toosendanin, showing dual positional activity with preference for C12-OH. 3. Kinetic, docking, MD, and mutagenesis analyses showed highest preference for compound 1 and identified catalytic/substrate-specificity residues including C32, H33, H164, E360 and W377. 4. MaAT8824 is more active than MaAT1704 and explains acetylated limonoid accumulation in chinaberry fruits.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in fruit; from fruit-correlated WGCNA module.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: In chinaberry, highly expressed in fruit.
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: downstream limonoid tailoring/acylation branch after azadirone-related intermediates
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers an acetyl group from acetyl-CoA to limonoid hydroxyl groups, preferentially acetylating C12-OH of compounds 1, 3 and 5, and also acetylating C3-OH of compound 6 to form toosendanin.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: terminal determinant for acetylated limonoid formation in chinaberry; key downstream tailoring step
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for metabolic engineering of limonoid acetylation patterns and production of bioactive toosendanin/isotoosendanin; could be stacked with upstream limonoid pathway genes.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: genome sequencing; transcriptome/WGCNA; metabolite profiling; Genome Sequence Archive CRA019071; Genome Warehouse PRJCA030181
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Accepts only acetyl-CoA among tested donors; narrow donor specificity and somewhat broader acceptor specificity. Tandem AT cluster on chromosome 3. Neo-functionalized relative to neem syntenic copy.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Melia azedarach; Nicotiana benthamiana; Escherichia coli
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes acetylated limonoid formation by catalysing mainly C12-OH acetylation and weaker C3-OH acetylation in chinaberry.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: WGCNA-guided candidate selection; heterologous expression in E. coli; in vitro enzyme assays; LC-MS/LC-MS-MS product identification; transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; tissue metabolite profiling; kinetic assays
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MaAT1704 acetylates the C12-OH of compounds 1 and 3 to produce toosendanin and isotoosendanin. 2. It also acetylates compound 5 to form compound 6 and can catalyse conversion of compound 6 to toosendanin via C3-OH acetylation, but with lower activity than MaAT8824. 3. Its activity supports the accumulation of compounds 2 and 4 in chinaberry fruits and indicates post-divergence neo-functionalization.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: In chinaberry, highly expressed in fruit.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers an acetyl group from acetyl-CoA to limonoid hydroxyl groups, preferentially acetylating C12-OH of compounds 1 and 3 and less efficiently catalysing C3-OH acetylation of compound 6.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: terminal determinant for acetylated limonoid formation in chinaberry; key downstream tailoring step
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for engineering acetylated limonoid profiles, though lower activity than MaAT8824 suggests MaAT8824 is preferable for engineering.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: genome sequencing; transcriptome/WGCNA; metabolite profiling; Genome Sequence Archive CRA019071; Genome Warehouse PRJCA030181
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Syntenic neem copies lacked activity; MaAT1704 belongs to the same expanded AT cluster on chromosome 3 and represents neo-functionalization after chinaberry-neem divergence.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Azadirachta indica; Nicotiana benthamiana; Escherichia coli
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Native AiAT0635 lacks detectable acetylation activity toward tested limonoid substrates, contributing to absence of C12-acetylated limonoids in neem; activity can be partially resurrected by six amino acid substitutions.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: synteny-guided candidate testing; heterologous expression in E. coli; in vitro enzyme assays; LC-MS; transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; domain/region swapping; site-directed mutagenesis; MD simulation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed candidate from neem tandem cluster; detailed tissue pattern not fully specified.
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: putative downstream tailoring step, but inactive in native form for tested reactions
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Native protein did not catalyse detectable acetyl transfer to the tested limonoid C12-OH substrates under assay conditions; engineered mutant gained weak activity producing toosendanin from compound 1 with acetyl-CoA.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: competes as inactive homolog / engineered version directly forms target product
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Provides a template for protein engineering to resurrect or introduce limonoid acetylation activity in neem or heterologous systems by editing a short N-terminal motif.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: genome sequencing; transcriptome; Genome Sequence Archive CRA019071; Genome Warehouse PRJCA030181
